org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism

Interface Graph<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>

  • Type Parameters:
    G - is the type of the graph.
    N - is the type of the node inside the graph.
    A - is the type of the anchors inside the graph.
    E - is the type of the node inside the graph.
    All Superinterfaces:
    Comparable<ModelObject>, ModelObject, Serializable
    All Known Implementing Classes:
    FiniteStateMachine, StandardGraph

    public interface Graph<G extends Graph<?,N,A,E>,N extends Node<G,?,A,E>,A extends Anchor<G,N,?,E>,E extends Edge<G,N,A,?>>
extends ModelObject
    This is the semantic object for graph models.
    Version:
    17.0 2013-10-23 21:02:58
    Author:
    Stéphane GALLAND
    Maven Group Id:
    org.arakhne.neteditor
    Maven Artifact Id:
    neteditor-formalism

    • Method Summary

      Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method and Description
      void addEdge(E edge)
      Add a Edge in this Graph
      void addNode(N node)
      Add a node in this Graph.
      void clear()
      Clear the graph content.
      Map<String,E> getEdgeByName()
      Get the edges classified by their names.
      E getEdgeByName(String name)
      Get the first edge that has the specified name.
      E getEdgeByUUID(UUID id)
      Get the edge that has the specified identifier.
      int getEdgeCount()
      Replies the number of edges in the graph.
      Set<String> getEdgeNames()
      Replies the names of the edges in a single collection.
      Set<E> getEdges()
      Get the edge's list of this Graph.
      Map<String,Set<E>> getEdgesByName()
      Get the edges classified by their names.
      Set<E> getEdgesByName(String name)
      Get the edges that have the specified name.
      Map<String,N> getNodeByName()
      Get the nodes classified by their names.
      N getNodeByName(String name)
      Get the first node that has the specified name.
      N getNodeByUUID(UUID id)
      Get the node that has the specified identifier.
      int getNodeCount()
      Replies the number of nodes in the graph.
      Set<String> getNodeNames()
      Replies the names of the nodes in a single collection.
      Set<N> getNodes()
      Get the node's list of this Graph
      Map<String,Set<N>> getNodesByName()
      Get the nodes classified by their names.
      Set<N> getNodesByName(String name)
      Get the nodes that have the specified name.
      boolean isEmpty()
      Replies if this graph has no node nor edge inside.
      int moveFromGraph(G graph)
      Move all the model objects from the specified graph to the current graph.
      int moveFromGraph(G graph, Set<UUID> identifiersToExtract)
      Move all the model objects from the specified graph to the current graph.
      void removeEdge(E edge)
      Remove a edge attached to this Graph.
      void removeNode(N node)
      Remove a node attached to this Graph All the link to other objects of the graph are also broking.

      • Methods inherited from interface org.arakhne.neteditor.formalism.ModelObject

        addModelObjectListener, findModelObject, fireComponentAdded, fireComponentRemoved, fireContainerChanged, fireContentChanged, fireLinkChanged, firePropertyChanged, getMetamodelSpecification, getName, getProperties, getUIEditableProperties, getUUID, getViewBinding, removeModelObjectListener, setName, setProperties, setUUID

      • Methods inherited from interface java.lang.Comparable

        compareTo

    • Method Detail

      • isEmpty

        boolean isEmpty()
        Replies if this graph has no node nor edge inside.
        Returns:
        true if the graph has no node nor edge inside; otherwise false.

      • getNodeCount

        int getNodeCount()
        Replies the number of nodes in the graph.
        Returns:
        the number of nodes in the graph.

      • getNodes

        Set<N> getNodes()
        Get the node's list of this Graph
        Returns:
        a list of Nodes.

      • getNodesByName

        Set<N> getNodesByName(String name)
        Get the nodes that have the specified name.
        Parameters:
        name -
        Returns:
        a list of Nodes.
        See Also:
        getNodeByName(String)

      • getNodeByUUID

        N getNodeByUUID(UUID id)
        Get the node that has the specified identifier.
        Parameters:
        id -
        Returns:
        the node or null

      • getNodeByName

        Map<String,N> getNodeByName()
        Get the nodes classified by their names. If two or more nodes have the same name, only one is replied.
        Returns:
        the map of the pairs name-node.

      • getNodesByName

        Map<String,Set<N>> getNodesByName()
        Get the nodes classified by their names.
        Returns:
        the map of the pairs name-nodes.

      • getNodeNames

        Set<String> getNodeNames()
        Replies the names of the nodes in a single collection.
        Returns:
        all the names of the nodes in this graph.

      • getNodeByName

        N getNodeByName(String name)
        Get the first node that has the specified name.
        Parameters:
        name -
        Returns:
        a list of Nodes.
        See Also:
        getNodesByName(String)

      • addNode

        void addNode(N node)
        Add a node in this Graph.
        Parameters:
        node - the new Node.

      • removeNode

        void removeNode(N node)
        Remove a node attached to this Graph All the link to other objects of the graph are also broking.
        Parameters:
        node - the Node to remove.

      • getEdgeCount

        int getEdgeCount()
        Replies the number of edges in the graph.
        Returns:
        the number of edges in the graph.

      • getEdges

        Set<E> getEdges()
        Get the edge's list of this Graph.
        Returns:
        a list of Edges.

      • getEdgeNames

        Set<String> getEdgeNames()
        Replies the names of the edges in a single collection.
        Returns:
        all the names of the edges in this graph.

      • getEdgesByName

        Set<E> getEdgesByName(String name)
        Get the edges that have the specified name.
        Parameters:
        name -
        Returns:
        a list of Edges.
        See Also:
        getEdgeByName(String)

      • getEdgeByUUID

        E getEdgeByUUID(UUID id)
        Get the edge that has the specified identifier.
        Parameters:
        id -
        Returns:
        the edge or null

      • getEdgeByName

        E getEdgeByName(String name)
        Get the first edge that has the specified name.
        Parameters:
        name -
        Returns:
        a list of Edges.
        See Also:
        getEdgesByName(String)

      • getEdgeByName

        Map<String,E> getEdgeByName()
        Get the edges classified by their names. If two or more edges have the same name, only one is replied.
        Returns:
        the map of the pairs name-edge.

      • getEdgesByName

        Map<String,Set<E>> getEdgesByName()
        Get the edges classified by their names.
        Returns:
        the map of the pairs name-edges.

      • addEdge

        void addEdge(E edge)
        Add a Edge in this Graph
        Parameters:
        edge - the new Edge.

      • removeEdge

        void removeEdge(E edge)
        Remove a edge attached to this Graph. All the link to other objects of the graph are also broking.
        Parameters:
        edge - the Edge to remove.

      • clear

        void clear()
        Clear the graph content. This function preserve the connections between the removed objects.

      • moveFromGraph

        int moveFromGraph(G graph)
        Move all the model objects from the specified graph to the current graph. This function preserve the connections between the moved objects.
        Parameters:
        graph - is the graph from which the objects should be moved.
        Returns:
        the number of objects that were moved.

      • moveFromGraph

        int moveFromGraph(G graph,
                Set<UUID> identifiersToExtract)
        Move all the model objects from the specified graph to the current graph. This function preserve the connections between the moved objects.
        Parameters:
        graph - is the graph from which the objects should be moved.
        identifiersToExtract - are the identifiers to extract and move into the current graph.
        Returns:
        the number of objects that were moved.
